title: Threat Hunting Outcomes Logged for Repeatable Hunts

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 RA-10 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) — Enterprise Security Content Update or custom KVStore
spl:
index=thunt sourcetype=hunt:record earliest=-90d
| stats values(hypothesis) as hyps, latest(outcome) as outcome, max(duration_min) as duration by hunt_id, hunter
| where outcome="inconclusive" OR duration>480
description: Structures hunt hypothesis, queries, and findings for RA-10 threat hunting (as organizational capability evidence).
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes thunt and sourcetypes hunt:record, hunt:record`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Require hunt closure fields; (2) link to IR tickets when escalated; (3) quarterly metrics for leadership; (4) library of proven SPL in repo outside Splunk. Indexes required: `index=thunt`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=hunt:record`, `sourcetype=hunt:record``.
visualization: Table (hunt backlog), Bar chart (outcomes), Single value (inconclusive %)
knownFalsePositives: Approved changes, scheduled maintenance, or documented exceptions can match the pattern; confirm with the change calendar and control owner before you escalate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) — Enterprise Security Content Update or custom KVStore.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=thunt` `sourcetype=hunt:record` HEC JSON.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require hunt closure fields; (2) link to IR tickets when escalated; (3) quarterly metrics for leadership; (4) library of proven SPL in repo outside Splunk.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=thunt sourcetype=hunt:record earliest=-90d
| stats values(hypothesis) as hyps, latest(outcome) as outcome, max(duration_min) as duration by hunt_id, hunter
| where outcome="inconclusive" OR duration>480
```

Understanding this SPL

**Threat Hunting Outcomes Logged for Repeatable Hunts (RA-10)** — Structures hunt hypothesis, queries, and findings for RA-10 threat hunting (as organizational capability evidence).

Documented **Data sources**: `index=thunt` `sourcetype=hunt:record` HEC JSON. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) — Enterprise Security Content Update or custom KVStore. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: thunt; **sourcetype**: hunt:record. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=thunt, sourcetype=hunt:record, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by hunt_id, hunter** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where outcome="inconclusive" OR duration>480` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (hunt backlog), Bar chart (outcomes), Single value (inconclusive %)
grandmaExplanation: We structures hunt hypothesis, queries, and findings for RA-10 threat hunting (as organizational capability evidence). That helps federal and agency security reviews match what the systems actually do day to day.
